comment_text.sn35.104:1.2: For “sanctuary from the yoke”, see note on SN 7.11:6.2.
comment_text.sn35.104:1.7: The duality between yoga (“yoke”) as “harness, fetter” and as “commitment to practice” is crucial to this sutta. The Buddha is not teaching nihilism, he is teaching us to “yoke” ourselves to the right cause.

root_text.sn35.104:1.5: Santi, bhikkhave, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.104:1.6: Te tathāgatassa pahīnā ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.sn35.104:1.7: Tesañca pahānāya akkhāsi yogaṁ, tasmā tathāgato ‘yogakkhemī’ti vuccati …pe…
root_text.sn35.104:1.8: santi, bhikkhave, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.104:1.9: Te tathāgatassa pahīnā ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.sn35.104:1.10: Tesañca pahānāya akkhāsi yogaṁ, tasmā tathāgato ‘yogakkhemī’ti vuccati.

translation_text.sn35.104:1.2: “Mendicants, I will teach you an exposition of the teaching, an explanation of one who has found sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.sn35.104:1.4: And what is an exposition of the teaching, an explanation of one who has found sanctuary from the yoke?
translation_text.sn35.104:1.5: There are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.104:1.6: The Realized One has given these up, cut them off at the root, made them like a palm stump, and obliterated them, so they are unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn35.104:1.7: He teaches yoking oneself to meditation for giving them up. That’s why the Realized One is called one who has found sanctuary from the yoke. …
translation_text.sn35.104:1.8: There are ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.104:1.9: The Realized One has given these up, cut them off at the root, made them like a palm stump, and obliterated them, so they are unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn35.104:1.10: He teaches yoking oneself to meditation for giving them up. That’s why the Realized One is called one who has found sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.sn35.104:1.11: This is an exposition of the teaching, an explanation of one who has found sanctuary from the yoke.”
